description: ISO 2768 general tolerances - structured rule set for Knowledge Base / Inference Engine use

size_range_convention.over: lower bound, exclusive (except where 'up_to' starts at 0.5)
size_range_convention.note: A dimension D falls in a range when over < D <= up_to

standards.title: General tolerances - Part 1: Tolerances for linear and angular dimensions without individual tolerance indications

applies_to: linear dimensions (external, internal, step, diameter, radius, distance, external radius, chamfer height)
angular dimensions (including right angles 90 degrees unless ISO 2768-2 is referenced; angles of uniform polygons)
linear and angular dimensions produced by machining assembled parts
does_not_apply_to: dimensions covered by other general-tolerance standards
theoretically exact dimensions in rectangular frames

table_1_linear_dimensions.title: Permissible deviations for linear dimensions except for broken edges

ranges.note: nominal sizes below 0.5 mm must be individually indicated

table_2_broken_edges.title: Permissible deviations for broken edges (external radii and chamfer heights)

ranges.note: below 0.5 mm must be individually indicated

table_3_angular_dimensions.title: Permissible deviations of angular dimensions
table_3_angular_dimensions.deviation_basis: applies to the length of the shorter side of the angle, in mm
notes: Angular general tolerances control only the general orientation of lines, not their form deviations.
General orientation is derived from the contacting line of ideal geometrical form (see ISO 8015).

rules.selection: The customary workshop accuracy must be considered when selecting the tolerance class.
rules.override: If a feature needs tighter or looser tolerance, indicate it individually adjacent to the nominal dimension.
rules.cast_vs_machined: For a dimension between an unfinished and a finished surface, the larger of the two applicable general tolerances applies (see ISO 8062 for castings).
rules.rejection: Workpieces exceeding the general tolerance shall not lead to automatic rejection, provided function is not impaired.

standards.title: General tolerances - Part 2: Geometrical tolerances for features without individual tolerance indications

applies_to: features produced primarily by removal of material
features that do not have individual geometrical tolerance indications

size_basis.flatness: longer lateral length of the surface, or diameter of the circular surface

circularity.narrative: The general tolerance on circularity is equal to the numerical value of the diameter tolerance, but in no case shall it be greater than the circular radial run-out value in Table 4.

cylindricity.narrative: Cylindricity has no direct general tolerance.

parallelism.narrative: General tolerance on parallelism equals the numerical value of the size tolerance or the flatness/straightness tolerance, whichever is greater.
parallelism.datum_selection: The longer of the two features is the datum; if equal length, either may be taken as datum.

perpendicularity.size_basis: nominal length of the shorter side
perpendicularity.datum_selection: The longer side is the datum.

symmetry.datum_selection: The longer of the two features is the datum.

coaxiality.narrative: Coaxiality has no direct general tolerance. Fallback: use circular radial run-out Table 4.

applies_to: run-out on any surface of revolution
circular_runout.datum_selection: Bearing surfaces if designated; otherwise the longer feature.

rules_and_principles.independency_principle_ISO_8015: General geometrical tolerances apply independently of the actual local size.
rules_and_principles.right_angles_override: The angular general tolerances in ISO 2768-1 do NOT apply to right angles (90 deg) when ISO 2768-2 is referenced.
rules_and_principles.rejection: Workpieces exceeding the general geometrical tolerance shall not lead to automatic rejection unless function is impaired.

rules: Place indication in or near the title block.

code_conflict_note.problem: The letter 'H' can mean both ISO 286 fit-class H AND ISO 2768-2 geometric tolerance class H.
code_conflict_note.project_convention: Use 'GH'/'GTH' for ISO 2768-2 class H in the GAI knowledge base.

default_values_for_missing_pmi.comment: If a feature has no PMI tolerance value and ISO 2768-mK is declared, resolve: look up feature type → size range → class K value.

datum_feature_with_no_tolerance.comment: Datum features with no tolerance indication: tolerance_value = 0, translation XYZ = 0, rotation = identity matrix I.

lookup_examples.description: Flatness of a 400mm x 400mm plate, ISO 2768-mK
Perpendicularity of a 150mm shorter side, class H
